name: SHL Occupational Personality Questionnaire (OPQ)
description: Get accurate, Insightful assessments of potential by matching individual working preferences directly to business requirements. Gain accurate, Insightful assessment of employee potential with SHL’s Occupational Personality Questionnaire. The OPQ helps you make more informed talent decisions. Participants answer questions about their working preferences to identify their strengths and areas for development within the universal competency framework. SHL quickly and reliably matches this understanding to a scientifically chosen profile, tailored to meet your hiring or development needs. Hiring managers receive a report on each candidate's fit, potential, and development gaps, integrated with SHL talent acquisition or management solutions and your company’s existing applicant tracking system. name: SHL Global Skills Assessment
description: 96 behavioral skills measured in 15 minutes. Objective, consistent skills data you can use from hiring decisions to development conversations to internal mobility. The SHL Global Skills Assessment gives organizations a consistent, science‑based view of behavioral skills across candidates and employees. It provides a shared language for understanding strengths, gaps, and the behaviors that drive performance. Because it’s built on one universal framework , the assessment creates a unified picture of skills across your organization. You can compare people fairly, understand capability at scale, and build a stronger foundation for development, mobility, and long‑term workforce planning. With one consistent framework before and after hire, you can use the SHL Global Skills Assessment in hiring, across your workforce, or both. Include the SHL Global Skills Assessment in role-specific hiring to identify your top candidates. Reuse their skills profiles at onboarding to guide targeted development from day one. Give the SHL Global Skills Assessment directly to employees. In 15 minutes per person, you gain organization-wide visibility into capabilities through detailed reporting. The assessment measures all 96 skills in SHL’s Universal Competency Framework, giving you a complete view of the workplace behaviors that drive performance across roles. In around 15 minutes, individuals compare short behavioral statements and select the ones most like them. This simple, forced‑choice format captures authentic workplace behavior while reducing response bias. The assessment produces a range of reports tailored to your use case. Individuals receive development insights they can use for onboarding or growth. HR teams can view results for single candidates or aggregated groups, and—when used for selection—generate structured interview guides or role‑aligned talent profiles. Alongside current strengths and gaps, the Reskilling Potential add‑on shows who has the willingness and ability to successfully upskill. This gives you a clearer view of who can grow into future roles, projects, and opportunities. Normed with global samples representative across job levels and geographies to enable consistent comparisons Built to support fair practices grounded in EEOC guidance for equal opportunity Make decisions you can defend because you're using validated methods with documented job relevance The Global Skills Assessment gives you a complete picture of your workforce’s behavioral skills. Our SHL AI Readiness Profile applies a focused lens to that data, revealing who’s ready to work effectively in an AI-powered environment and where to build capability next. Most organizations have multiple frameworks—one for hiring, another for performance, a third for leadership. The UCF gives you a single, validated framework that works across all of them. You can map your existing frameworks to the UCF (we help with this), which means you finally get consistent, comparable data. Plus, the UCF’s Skills Taxonomy is normed globally and mapped to 600+ O*NET profiles and 55,000 job titles, something custom frameworks can't match. The GSA reveals individual strengths and skills gaps, making it clear where development is needed. It also identifies employees with high adaptability and ability to learn, those most ready to reskill into new roles as business needs evolve. This turns skills data into targeted development actions at individual, team, and organizational levels. Skills data collected during hiring remain accessible throughout the employee lifecycle. The same objective profile that informed the hiring decision becomes the foundation for onboarding conversations, development planning, and internal movement—no repeat assessment required. Yes. 15+ independent validation studies across industries show GSA composites consistently predict supervisor-rated performance and productivity. Global norms allow comparison across job levels, roles, and geographies using the same benchmark—whether you're assessing 50 people in one location or 50,000 across a global workforce. Make smarter hiring decisions with skills data that you can carry forward through the employee lifecycle. Uncover skills gaps and build targeted development plans that align to business objectives. Use objective skills data to match employees to the right internal opportunities, and vice versa.

name: Universal Competency Framework (UCF)
description: Competencies are ‘sets of behaviors that are instrumental in the delivery of desired results’ (Bartram et al, 2002). In the business context they are behaviors that support the attainment of objectives within the context of that specific organization’s strategy. Competencies are sometimes also called ‘capabilities’ but irrespective of the term used, the focus is on ‘behaviors’ not technical knowledge. The Universal Competency Framework (UCF) provides a rational, consistent, and practical basis for understanding people’s behaviors at work and their likelihood of succeeding in certain roles and environments. We constructed the UCF from our wide-reaching program of research on workplace behaviors, so it can be used to build competency models for all types of jobs or to map existing models onto a universal set of competency components. We also provide a full suite of talent management tools and content to support the UCF including APTA™, our patented, customizable personnel psychological testing tool for directly measuring the UCF competencies most important for success in a role. Additional tools include questionnaire items, behavioral anchors, interview questions, and assessment exercises for employee selection and development. These provide the source materials for building assessment programs. Most importantly, each competency component is linked to our portfolio of assessments . A competency framework allows organizations to align individual behaviors to group or corporate goals. It specifies the requirements for good performance in a given role, thereby facilitating end-to-end talent management practices, from identifying job-relevant competencies to engaging talent assessment tools to managing performance. The competency framework can help you to easily understand the areas of strength and improvement within the whole organization across various departments and locations. The UCF is defined in terms of a 3-tier structure: Statistical analysis was progressed to inform this structure and to ensure the competencies at all levels are discrete. Our Science team is constantly innovating and pushing the boundaries of what is deemed possible. Their latest innovation is Universal Competency Assessment that can measure all 96 components of Universal Competency Framework in just 15 minutes! Learn more about their work in our new innovation powerhouse, SHL Labs . Learn the science and structure behind the UCF framework and how organizations can use it to map all job requirements all across levels, job families, and roles-together with example applications. The guide also includes best practices to thoughtfully implement, or review, competency frameworks, with seven key questions and practical tips to put them into action. talent acquisition and talent management solutions, maximize the potential of your company’s greatest asset—your people.

name: SHL Realistic Job Previews
description: Preview roles and fit with realistic job previews. SHL’s experts develop scenarios that best explain and showcase each job opportunity. Realistic Job and Culture Previews (RJP) feature customized questions and interactive multimedia, fully branded to your brand. Candidates are immersed in three or four (typically) realistic scenarios, choosing the response they feel is most appropriate to them. Candidates receive immediate feedback on their suitability. If they believe they are a good fit, they can proceed to the next stage of their hiring journey with your company. Learn how scenarios can help candidates understand your role and organization better. Maximize the potential of your company’s greatest asset—your people.

name: SVAR Assessment:Measure Real Spoken Language, at Scale
description: Assess genuine language skills across speaking, vocabulary, grammar, listening and comprehension — giving you a reliable signal you can trust in high-volume hiring. SHL SVAR is our automated spoken-language assessment designed to measure real communication ability. It evaluates how candidates speak and converse to give you a clear, evidence‑based view of workplace‑ready communication. SVAR supports 20+ languages and 7 accents , works on mobile and web , and takes just 12–15 minutes to complete. AI‑powered scoring delivers fast, consistent results so hiring teams can assess spoken‑language capability at scale. SHL SVAR measures the essentials of spoken communication in one quick assessment; how clearly candidates speak, their pronunciation and fluency, how well they understand and respond to information, and how accurately they use vocabulary and grammar. And because it captures spontaneous, real-time speech, you get a genuine view of a candidate's spoken ability rather than a rehearsed response. Automated scoring delivers fast, consistent results, helping you screen high-volume and multilingual talent pools with confidence. It’s designed to be simple for candidates and efficient for hiring teams. The result: a fast, transparent and evidence‑based view of spoken language capability. SHL SVAR combines globally recognized language standards with AI-driven speech analysis to deliver reliable, scalable spoken language assessment. Clear communication leads to better experiences and stronger relationships Shared language standards support smoother internal and external communication Accurate assessment means hiring candidates with the right language skills from day one Consistent language standards across international operations SHL SVAR is built for roles where spoken communication directly impacts performance, customer experience, and service quality. SHL SVAR helps teams quickly identify candidates who can communicate clearly, confidently, and effectively from day one. Fill your talent pools with the skills of the future to ensure your workforce can flex as their environments change. Find candidates at scale that will quickly make an impact and grow with your organization. Accurately assess critical skills such as customer service, communication and language capabilities to identify candidates with the right skills and fit for BPO roles – while managing high volumes and controlling costs. Customer care roles can be demanding and it takes a specific skillset to succeed. Identify candidates with the skills to deliver excellent customer service and adapt in a fast-changing contact center environment.
